title: 💔대문자 T와 F가 함께 고민 상담해주면 벌어지는 일 | 진주랑 연애 고민상담소
text:
["T"]
When I read stories like this I'm like, is that really necessary? ["F"]
But we have to give advice so we have to empathize. [I just can't relate, what do I do?]
Why? If I can't relate I can still say things as is. But this person must be frustrated.
They sent this and you're like "I don't know why I should do that." - That's me?
- Yes. ["T" Woo Jae and "F" Jin Joo]
All cold alone. I don't know why I should do that. [Today's Joo WooJae] [Adapted from a real submission]
Alright, we'll do [from previous relationship counseling]
some relationship counseling together. [Jin Joo brought cakes and candles]
Can I ask what the candles are for? [Celebrating this moment together]
They're to celebrate our meeting. Hmm. [Even though we see each other every week?]
It's already been years, though. Still, every time feels new and special. - So you're celebrating today's... meeting.
- We're celebrating today. Or maybe we're celebrating me coming back here. Your comeback? Cheers to Jin Joo's comeback. - Happy comeback to Jin Joo
- Happy comeback Happy comeback to Jin Joo Happy comeback dear Jin Joo comeback Happy comeback to Jin Joo Thanks, guys. Thanks, guys. You blow out the bear one. One, two, three.

title: 💔대문자 T와 F가 함께 고민 상담해주면 벌어지는 일 | 진주랑 연애 고민상담소
text:
ack? Cheers to Jin Joo's comeback. - Happy comeback to Jin Joo
- Happy comeback Happy comeback to Jin Joo Happy comeback dear Jin Joo comeback Happy comeback to Jin Joo Thanks, guys. Thanks, guys. You blow out the bear one. One, two, three. [What are you doing...] A bite for the Joo-holders and one for me. Let's enjoy this while we [Woo Jae's Relationship Counseling with Jin Joo]
go through your lovely relationship troubles. - It's like your main content, right?
- Totally! Some videos do especially well on our channel. This is one of those. - Think today's will blow up too?
- Of course. I mean, I'm so good at this. [Joo-rcissist]
It's gonna blow up thanks to me. [Yeah, sure...] [First story]
Title. I confessed and got rejected. - Hello, Joo Woo Jae and Jin Joo.
- Hello. - I'm a guy in my mid twenties.
- Hi. I confessed to someone recently and got rejected. The thing is, we work
in the same company, same team. Whoa. - Seeing her every day is driving me nuts.
- Oh no! [Jin Joo, please]
Can you be quiet? The story... I'm reading the story here. Sorry, yeah. It's really must be driving him nuts. The most important thing now
is to share the information. [Sorry]
Right. I still have feelings, and it's super awkward. So I take different routes just to avoid her. - I can't act like nothing happened.
- Hmm. But I also wanna get back to normal.
What should I do? Also, how do I move on? I think this person is now... umm... in a total fog, like zero visibility.
Just emotional fine dust everywhere. [Harsh] Sorry, that was harsh from the start. Usually when people confess they scope it out first. You know when you toss a sock into the laundry basket [This one's going in for sure]
and you just know it's going in? That's what a good confession feels like. But it sounds like our guy just aimed blindly into a tangle of vines. It's like a total laser field out there [Maybe he was reckless in confessing?]
and he just tried to cross it. [Stop assuming]
But hey, who knows? We don't know what the other person was like. [What if she flirted first?]
Maybe she was being too kind. If she was being kind only to me, all the time. Then I might be like, huh? [Should I toss the sock?]
I might start wondering. But for most people, when you receive one kindness [Kindness]
just like that you look around, like [Do you like me?]
"What does this mean?" You can't do that. We don't know what exactly happened. Right, we don't know the situation. Anyway, he confessed and got rejected. Now the real issue is they still work together on the same team. [How to act normal?]
What do you do to just go about things like normal? Right, if they're
on the same team, in a same company he should've asked "What should I do?" first [He went straight for the confession]
before he confessed. - But he already confessed.
- That's done now. My first piece of advice is... Let's say you accidentally let out a fart. You might start squeaking things on purpose [Camouflage]
like this to cover it. So in front of her just start flirting all over the place. [Speechless]
That's a way. Like that's just who you are. Right. [Time for real advice now]
For me if I really liked that person I'd want him to know it wasn't just some light thing. Oh. I'd call him out and be honest... [No way]
Call him out? Yeah! But in a non-pressuring way
I think I could do that. [Straightforward approach]
"I'm sorry I confessed. I hope we can get along." Be upfront and let them know
you're not expecting anything else. [That's how I can get over it too!]
Help put both of your minds at ease. Honestly, I think that's the best approach too. But the reason I didn't bring it up earlier is this guy's clearly [Ongoing]
not over it. And when you still have feelings you can't say things like "I'm sorry for making things uncomfortable." It's just so hard. So I'd say go big. If they turned you down? Still be super kind and act like nothing happened. You know what happens
when someone knows you like them. - It gets to you.
- Totally. Right. Be super nice to them for a while. Then suddenly turn cold. "Yes? Can I help you?" "Let's keep it to work talk from now on." Then she's going to be like [What's he doing?]
"What's going on with him?" So what I mean is once things get awkward like this you need to shamelessly power through it. [Go full-on shameless mode]
Whatever the method, just go bold. And you're in your mid twenties? That's the time to go all in. Don't let one rejection get you down. - Start looking around.
- To other places? This is when your emotions run wild anyway. Honestly, the best way to get over someone
is with someone else. - Exactly.
- In your mid twenties... - Go check out some bars or something.
- Hehe. And when someone new comes along - you don't even need to move on.
- Of course. [Love heals best with new love]
Because it'll be full of that person anyway. You forget her in an instant. So, we've got three options here. [Jin Joo's suggestion]
1. Be honest like me and just talk it out. Second one, like you said... [Woo Jae's suggestion]
Be shameless and go cold [2. Turn into a shameless guy]
make people think you're weird. Just like this. "I only care about work." Act like that kind of person. [Workaholic mode]
Like that. 3. Find someone new Hit up some bars or wherever. Basically, this is all part of life. It's totally normal. Whether it's school, work, anywhere. When you confess in a community
getting rejected is more likely than not. [This is all completely normal]
It's only natural to feel awkward afterward. Just know this and think
"Oh, I went through something totally normal." Totally. [Think of it as a valuable experience]
This experience is going to help you grow a lot when it comes to love and relationships. [Next story]
Title. My boyfriend has dead fish eyes. - What's her personality like?
- Hahaha. [Joking]
Sounds like someone I want nothing to do with. Hello. [Reality check] You sound like you'd be good at giving injections. Hahaha. Poke! [Ignoring]
Hello. I've been dating my boyfriend
for a year and a half now. Jin Joo, could you lower your script a bit? You're blocking your face. Where should I start? Let's go from the top. Title! My boyfriend has dead fish eyes. [Ouch]
She just said lower the script. [Bickering]
She literally just told you to put it down. Am I supposed to lower it all the way here? [Final attempt]
Hello. I'm in my mid twenties, and have been
dating my boyfriend for a year and a half. Lately, he's completely stopped showing affection. It made me feel really hurt
so recently I brought it up. I asked him to tell me he loves me sometimes
that he misses me or even ask me how I'm doing. And you know what he said? Hmph! "We've been dating for over 500 days.
Of course I might run out of questions." "If I didn't care
would I text you at every meal?" That's what he said. I was so shocked, we ended up fighting. And during the argument, he said "I think we just have different ways of loving." "Let's break up." He said he tried, but it didn't feel like enough. So he wanted to break up. I don't think that's different ways of loving. I think it's more like different sizes of love.
What do you think? Also, when I asked him not to break up
he agreed to stay. But for a whole month now
he hasn't once texted me first. He only replies when I message him.
It's frustrating. Even when we meet, he's got that dead fish stare. This whole "different ways of loving" thing I think his love just faded I don't think it has anything to do
with ways of loving. His love just faded. At that point, you try to fix things, sure. [It's not working. It is what it is]
If it's not working, you have to accept it. For her, there's just no acceptance. [You can never change!]
In her mind, this guy has to stay with her. It's not about accepting really... She's not even aware that's where things are. Because that's her way of loving. "If I didn't care, would I text you every meal?" When I heard this [The boyfriend's side]
I actually get what he means. But she heard that and was shocked. [Maybe it's just a personality difference?]
Then maybe they're just not compatible. So! Maybe his love didn't really fade. [Split opinions]
Nah, I think it's faded. No, I mean... Maybe during the fight, his feelings died. But I don't think he'd already checked out before that. I think only the other person can know that for sure. You can kind of sense it. It really shows. At times like those you know those demands trying to change a person? The moment you start making those [I don't even want to look at you!]
I think the other person will pull away further. [Listen to me]
It could sound like a demand but it could be a negotiation
trying to find a middle ground. [Do you really need to negotiate love?]
"Say you love me and you miss me." I don't think this is negotiation. Personally, I'm the type that barely says "I love you." I can totally feel it. If I can feel that I'm loved and cared for that's enough for me. That's why, when I see stories like this I'm like, "Is this necessary?" But we're here to give advice so we have to empathize. [But what if I can't empathize though]
Why? If I can't relate I should just say things as is. Imagine how frustrating this must be for her.
She sends us a story and you're like
"I don't know why I should do that." Me? [Yes, you]
Yes. [No heart]
So cold like this. - Bla bla bla.
- Oh. [Doppelgänger]
Like this. This type of person often thinks "Everyone must think like I do." [Therefore]
I'm just saying what I think. - She needs to know...
- There are people like this. There are people like this. Maybe she should try thinking
about things in this way. [I get what you're saying]
Some people are like you, sure. But I'm here to empathize. So I'm trying to do that. [I'm going home]
Then do the show alone. Why are you being so emotional? [Let's stop fighting] Let's be real. She's in her mid twenties. Dating for 500 days in her age? That's actually a solid relationship. In this fast-paced world. People date for a week and call it love. So 500 days is pretty impressive. If his love has faded enough for her to notice it might be time to accept it. [Sometimes]
Experience that breakup as well. [you need to face the end]
Go through the process. [Second opinion]
"If I didn't care would I text you every meal?" These words might actually be how he feels. [Everyone loves differently]
Maybe this really is his way of loving. So maybe take this opportunity
to realize there are people like this. [Maybe just think about this person]
And reflect on how grateful you are [as a different human being?]
for the love you did receive. I'm not gonna ask you to break up. You will break up anyway. [So much to say, but I'll hold it back] Let's move on. Title. A wrongful encounter. Hello. I have an ex-boyfriend I broke up with
about five years ago. We broke up
because of his female friends and cheating. So we ended things on very bad terms. Here's the story. A while ago, one of my friends
told me she got picked up on the street. She was all giddy
showing off how excited she was. I thought it was cute
and sincerely congratulated her. But then it turns out the guy who got her number was my ex. [No way] Can you believe this? It's been ages since we broke up and my friend didn't even know
what he looked like. [Glancing around] What? What's the issue here? [Hey, please]
Let's just hear the rest of it. Sorry. I couldn't just sit back and watch my friend
get involved with that guy. So I told her, "That guy's my ex." "I'm saying this for your sake." "I really hope you don't talk to him." [Glaring] "I have no interest in dating my friend's ex." "I'll take care of it." So I trusted her. But after that, her texts got sparse. Even when I reached out
she'd just give half-hearted replies. The answer's clear, right? They started dating, obviously. I felt uneasy, so I brought it up
with another friend. She said "Hmm... I think she's seeing someone these days?" "Maybe that's why she's been busy." - Seriously?
- Wow. I'm not on Instagram, so I checked her story
through another friend's account. She'd posted photos of herself
out on a date with my ex. I have zero lingering feelings for that guy. We're done, and I know
I have no right to interfere. But the fact that my friend lied to me it just really hurts and I'm mad. So what should I do? Is cutting her off the answer? Hmm... That's the story. This one's a one-second decision. - Cut off the friend?
- Yes. If she didn't even know who your ex was [Maybe not that close]
then you two probably weren't that close anyway. You're already strangers. I mean, would I ever go to my friend and say "Hey, is it cool if I date your ex?" [When we're not even that close]
No need. Of course, that's not necessary. [Why did she say she'd take care of it?]
But she didn't have to say that she'll take care of it. Like, what was she supposed to say then? "Oh really? I want to check things out though." That's basically the same
as saying she wants to date him. And that whole "I'll take care of it" it can mean two things. [Get rid of him]
I'll take care of him. I'll take care of him. [As my boyfriend]
I'll take care of him. Right. That's possible. [Being honest]
Even if she said something honest it would've only led to drama. If she already made up her mind she could totally say
"I'll just handle it myself." [No need to say they're dating]
I don't think she has to tell her friend. I guess it depends on how deep that friendship was. You know what I think? [The friend ignored her advice]
Their trust is already broken. That's why I'm saying this. I don't think [First time agreeing]
they were ever that close to begin with. - Right, I think so, too.
- That's my guess. Honestly, if they were really close friends
that "Should I cut her off?" question would be way more painful. I'd be like what should we do... But it didn't sound like that. [Cutting her off might not be bad]
Just go ahead and end things, I guess. If she was really a friend she would've at least said something. And sorry to say this but I think you're under the impression that you're the one holding the scissors here. When in reality she already cut ties with you
the second she started dating him. [Sad but true]
Yeah, it does feel like that. I don't know why she thinks
she's holding the knife. [That's so harsh]
Why do you have to stab her again when she's already hurt? [It's frustrating]
Listen, instead of wasting time on this stuff... [1. Go find someone you actually like] [2. Hug] [3. Make out] You never know. Maybe she already has a boyfriend anyway. And she said "I don't have any feelings left." So this is more about the trust with her friend. [Smart Jin Joo]
I think that's the main concern. So let aside the ongoing relationship... The fact that the friend
didn't tell her till the end living her best life out there... [Bye]
Let her live it. Be quick. Hurry up! [Cutting her off is a waste of time]
Don't even think about cutting her off. [Focus on your life!] [Next story]
Title. I opened Pandora's Box. Greetings. I've been dating my girlfriend for two years. It took me a long time to win her over. Thankfully, I could finally
call her my girlfriend. Back then, I was really happy. Of course, I'm still happy now, and I do love her. I'm really torn about whether I should
marry my girlfriend these days. The reason is that I opened Pandora's box
early on in our relationship. Inside that box was 80 million won in debt. Honestly, back then, I didn't think much of it. I believed love could conquer everything. But now that marriage feels real I've come to realize
just how swept up in emotion I was. What's worse is that
she never brought it up herself. I found out by chance and even after that
she still seemed to avoid the topic entirely. If I had at least seen her
trying to reduce the debt I think it would've eased my concerns. But instead, she sends me
links to clothes, saying "This is cute!" And she talks about cosmetic procedures.
It's hard to take. Should you be willing to handle
something like this if you love someone? Or is this one of those problems
love alone can't fix? I mentally checked out halfway through this one. I felt that. [Seven years of Love Naggers experience]
Still, you read the whole story. - I was a pro, right?
- Yes. [What if the debt's still there]
What if this girlfriend [but she showed her effort?]
had at least shown some effort? [Refer to 2:32 timestamp]
I always say this even when we talk about things
like splitting the bill. If she just said something like "Oh, let me..." If she just did this much... [Even the smallest gesture changes everything]
I wouldn't mind paying at all. But if she stands there at the register like... [Princess mode] If she's like that it starts to feel unfair. So, like you just asked - if she just showed some silent effort...
- Even if she didn't say anything. If I saw her effort and I'm the only one who knew about the debt that would actually make me [Triggering fatherly instincts]
want to pay it off for her. You'd feel sympathetic, right. Maybe she has paid it back though. - Oh, the girlfriend.
- Yes, this one... - He found out at the start of this relationship.
- Right, so it's been two years. I think a proper fact-check is necessary. Let's say you find out [If she goes that far]
that she's using an overdraft account to pay for skincare, clothes and bags. You need to turn around [Run away]
and run as fast as you can. But he said he chased after her for a long time. "I worked so hard to get this person."
Just because of this you have to overlook how she's handling her debt? [Nope]
I don't think so. Let's say everything else about her is perfect. If she vanished tomorrow your world would go dark. Okay, Jin Joo, look. - Let's say we're playing a game.
- Yes. With a joystick. And we yank out the handle. - Can we play the game?
- No. - No, right?
- No. Same thing. Just because everything else is fine [Super logical]
it doesn't mean that thing works. [Speechless] Why are you glaring at me? Haha. [Because I'm an "F"]
Still... we should consider how he feels. Well then... ["Aww, you poor thing" on loop]
just record "Aww, you poor thing" and let him play that on loop. But the fact that he sent in this long story it means he's really struggling. But then from our perspective [Joystick blah blah, handle blah blah]
if we're just like "joystick, blah blah" if you're gonna be like that... What's this? - What is this show for then?
- This is... This is the frame. [Unfair]
Look, I was so emotionally invested! I felt for this person, you know? And I'm saying I want to feel
even deeply for this person. The answer is actually out. Yeah, that answer came out from my mouth. I agreed with you, which is why I glared at you. Because I had nothing to say to that. But I just wanted to add something. How do I give this person [This is what being an "F" looks like]
a little more emotional comfort? [Nope]
If you comfort him and he ends up marrying this person then what?
How are you going to be responsible for his life? - I can't.
- You can't, right? So now he needs to check the debt again. If the debt's the same or worse I'd just be disappointed
at her attitude towards her debt. [Farewell] Still, I think you have a serious talk with her. For all we know
maybe she is trying to pay it off right now. We never know. Also, to be fair if it's debt for a house that's a completely different story. [Make sure you talk about the debt clearly!]
That's why you need a conversation. So check the facts. [Final story]
Title. My boyfriend is too controlling. - Hello.
- Hehe. I'm a college student in my early twenties. I've been dating my boyfriend
for about a year now. At first, we were just close friends. But I grew fond of him
because he was sweet and considerate. Now, we're officially a couple. Lately though, I feel like
there's a crack forming in our relationship. Whenever he goes out drinking
or hangs out with friends he always sends me photos. I figured, "Oh, he's just being thoughtful" and didn't think much of it. But now, when I go out with my friends
he insists that I send him pictures too. So I did that a few times. But recently, it's gotten worse. Even after I send a photo he asks me to send another one, 20 minutes later. And when I say, "Didn't I just send you one?" He goes, "What, you can't even do that?
Are you hiding something?" He keeps nagging me and sometimes he even scolds me. Even when my friends are right beside me he keeps calling me on FaceTime. It's so embarrassing, I've had to apologize
and leave early more than once. - Ptooey!
- I can't drink so when I go out, we just eat and grab coffee. I don't understand why he doesn't trust me. He says it's because he's worried about me. But to me, it just feels suffocating now. Is there a way to fix his controlling behavior? Ugh. Great. Now I'm annoyed to end on this note. What kind of person are you when you're dating? Depends on how long we've been dating. Early on, sure, sharing photos is cute. [Totally normal in the beginning]
Sure. But after some time I think it's better to just give each other space. Here, they've been dating for a year. And in your twenties... at that age I think it was pretty intense for me as well. Getting obsessed? Maybe not obsessed but I did feel upset a lot. You don't really know what love is at that age. So your entire mind [All my thoughts were you]
is filled with that one person. But no one can give all of their life to you. You can't either. We're all living in society, after all. Now that we're older
we've got a more rational mindset. Didn't you go through this in your early twenties? In my early twenties let me tell you what happened
between me and the person back then. [College love story incoming]
I was in college. She wanted to come to my school
the day before my exam. And I was like, "Uh... I've got an exam tomorrow." [Me or the exam?]
"So what's more important? Me, or your exam?" So I ended up not studying, met up with her [Retook the class]
and I failed the exam. Back then, I didn't know any better. So I met up with her but I couldn't understand her. The exam is one day but the relationship continues, right? After that I started to feel resistance to those
"just you and me in our little world" [It's not healthy relationships]
types of relationships. I've always been thinking that. - You just need someone who matches your style.
- Exactly. It's not about who's right or wrong. But clearly, these two aren't a good match. [Main question here]
"Can I fix his obsession?" That's the question. He's just going to suppress it. [Changing a personality is hard]
He's not going to change anytime soon. For us, being in a relationship is like... We don't burn with passion anymore. We're just like this. Calm. Cold and composed. - Jin Joo, sit up straight.
- Why me... hehe. Just match our age. Cold. [Jin Joo learns something weird]
Head cold, heart warm. If this was younger me
I'd be crying my eyes out over these. But I've been through enough to know better. That's a bit disappointing. We brought you here to cry your eyes out. If you've lost that you're breaking our contract. Oh, really? Was there a contract I missed? Verbal contracts are still contracts, you know. [Clash of the titans]
You didn't give me any verbs, so what contract? [Conclusion]
Anyway, rather than trying to fix your boyfriend... Actually, I'd rather you not try to fix anyone. - Honestly, yeah.
- Humans... You just learn to suppress it. The best quote I learned
from seven years of Love Naggers is this. You can't change a person. [Relating]
You replace them. If someone could be fixed
just because you wanted them to be... I don't think those people exist. You're right. We should've been more empathetic. [You can't fix someone's personality]
But I'm actually a really logical person, so... Sure, I know what love feels like. Being head over heels. But if that turns into some fantasy where you trap yourselves
on a tiny planet like B612 and cut yourself off from the world... [Stop this relationship]
That kind of love is toxic. [Today's Joo WooJae] All right. So, how were the stories today? Was it fun? It's been a while since I felt
that young, passionate kind of love. [Teasing]
It's been ages, right? I kind of felt my heart heating up today. What kind of relationship would you want? [A love you'd actually want to try?]
A love you'd want to try. From these stories? What are you even saying? Alright, then I'll go with [Mr. Dead Fish Eyes picked]
the dead fish eyes. I actually want to try the Pandora's Box one. I'm curious. I want to know how that debt turned out. So you take the debt I'll take the dead fish eyes. If someone texts me at every meal? I'd consider that real dedication. Honestly, I'd be grateful. I'll take care of this relationship really well. If you're grateful
for getting a text every mealtime [We're grateful for that]
that's what gratitude looks like at our age. So for those of you out there
even if things feel painful now there'll come a day, like us... Calm. You'll know what real love is. Calm. You know what this means? - It means you're unshaken.
- Exactly. Shin Hyun Joon. The snowstorm. Calm. This calm state we're in... We've just been through it
earlier than those younger folks. Right. It's a good place to be, honestly. After all the crying and drama, everything... [Weren't you a "T"-minator?]
Have you ever cried over someone, though? Of course. There was a time I broke up
with someone I really loved. I was vacuuming at home and suddenly my face just twisted - and the tears started flowing.
- Oh. That happened. Also, this one's a famous story.
Back in high school [Which is why I'm this skinny now]
I didn't eat lunch for two weeks. So, it's all those moments that brought you here. Honestly, I thought you were just... - Calm.
- just... born like this. You don't get born like this. It's all from experience. Anyway, thank you so much. - Shall we wrap it up now?
- Yeah. I had a great time today. "Today's Joo WooJae." Thank you for always being here. - There's the door right there.
- Yes. Are you kicking me out? Yes. How can you treat a guest like this? [Playful]
Because it's over. Oh, right. We forgot our signature sign-off for the channel. [Today's outfit reveal]
We need to share what you're wearing. The comments are gonna ask "Jin Joo, where's that vest from?" [It's sponsored, so I don't know]
What if I don't know? Oh, your stylist... You do it, I'll just watch. [Because it's a fashion channel, after all]
Let me handle business for a second. So, this jacket I'm wearing is from a brand called FRAGE.
I actually showed a leather jacket before. They apparently saw that and improved it a lot. So this is probably the new jacket
from their current season. The T-shirt underneath - is from RYOO.
- RYOO. The pants are from COOR. They're from like 2 or 3 years ago. You know those baggy jeans
that are trending on Pinterest? I don't have many of those.
so I dug around at home and found these. They've got that perfect slouchy fit. Just like this. It's really not over the top. Shoes are 4-hole derby shoes from PENNON. They've been doing this design
since their first season, one of their staple. - Alright, look over here.
- Right. - You know the sign-off, right?
- Yes. Everyone, stay well out there, alright?
